ops.title: How the cc-context ccx guard pack is wired to fire, plus capt-hook --hooks / packs.toml / SessionStore scoping gotchas
ops.body:
The cc-context ccx guard pack (`plugin/hooks/*.py`) fires via `uvx capt-hook --hooks <dir> run <Event>`: `plugin/hooks/hooks.json` (auto-discovered Claude Code plugin hook manifest) points at `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks` for plugin consumers, and `.claude/settings.json` points at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/plugin/hooks` for this dev repo. Before this wiring landed (2026-07), the pack fired NOWHERE — the repo-root `.claude/capt-hook.toml` is a pack-*publishing* manifest, not a load trigger.

Gotchas (capt-hook 4.0.0):
- `--hooks <dir>` overrides only the hooks DIR; it does NOT suppress `.claude/hooks/packs.toml` pack discovery — both load together. So wire ONE `--hooks` entry per event; do NOT add a `--hooks` entry *alongside* a bare `uvx capt-hook run` entry or every marketplace pack double-fires.
- `discover_hooks` skips `test_*.py`/`conftest`/`*/tests/*` (loader.is_test_module) and `__capt_hook_skip__ = True` modules, and catches per-module import errors — so a pytest file can safely live in the hooks dir.
- `SessionStore`/`WorkflowState` is per-session AND per-hook-name — it can't carry state from one hook to another or across sessions. For cross-hook/cross-session state (e.g. the learned-JSON-shapes nudge) write your own JSON at `resolve_state_dir()/…` (honors `$CAPTAIN_HOOK_STATE_DIR`) with atomic replace + filelock.

Related: [[cc-context-architecture]], [[capt-hook-local-testing]].
